turns.content: Why does this even work this way? I'm trying to parse the Authorization header in my side project and I feel like I just pasted a bunch of boilerplate code into the router instead of actually doing anything useful.

It's frustrating when new crates make things more verbose than they need to be. The good news is that TypedHeader from the headers crate solves this by letting you define custom header types and then use axum's extractor pattern to pull them out cleanly without manual parsing or regex hacks every time.
Okay, so I'm defining a struct with a derives macro for serde and then using it as a type in the handler? But what happens if I try to extract something that doesn't match my TypedHeader definition?

If you define a custom header type that doesn't correspond to an actual incoming request, axum will just skip it or return None depending on how you configure the extraction logic. The headers crate ensures your struct matches what's actually in the request, so the typed extractor only cares about valid types and ignores anything else.
